[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)

- Description: Edgar->dao_protocol: activate PR6b in production + verify PR6a payment path via Stripe sandbox
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/agentic_ai_context/pull/219
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/sentiment_importer/pull/1070
  
  Details:
  Activated PR6b in production and verified the PR6 payment paths. Deployed the webhook delegation to seni_ror_new (git pull of #1070 -- brought only the 5 migration commits, no Gemfile change) and restarted the seni_ror systemd unit; confirmed Edgar healthy afterward (browser UA -> 200 on /users/sign_in + homepage; curl 401 is the expected bot-rate-limit filter, not a regression) and that the ramped :8010 routes kept serving through the restart. Verified the PR6b delegation wiring with a synthetic checkout.session.completed to Rails /stripe_webhook: Rails delegated to the dao_protocol service (POST /stripe/order_sync from 127.0.0.1, journal-confirmed) and still acked 200 via the rescue (fake session -> no write). Verified PR6a's MINTED->Stripe-checkout path against a Stripe test-mode sandbox: spun up a 2nd dao_protocol instance on :8011 using the sk_test key from Edgar's development.rb, found a MINTED code, confirmed a 302 redirect to checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_... (no SOLD write since we stop at the redirect), then tore the sandbox down. All 6 routes now cut over and PR6b live. Remaining is operator-driven: an optional real-payment reconcile->SOLD test and PR7 cleanup.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
